channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Hannah Pingree and Bobby Charles Will Face Off in Maine Governor’s Race
item.description: Mr. Charles, a conservative Republican, had led the field ahead of a ranked-choice runoff. But Ms. Pingree, a former Maine House speaker, had been in a tight race with other Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell

item.title: Matt Dunlap Wins Democratic House Primary in Maine’s Swing District
item.description: Mr. Dunlap, a progressive Democrat, is set to face former Gov. Paul LePage, a Republican, in a competitive general election in November.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: Graeme Sloan/Getty Images
item.media:description: Matt Dunlap, the state auditor, was the Maine secretary of state from 2013 to 2021.

item.title: San Francisco Film Patrons Are Found Dead on Side of Highway
item.description: Judith Sheldon, 84, daughter of an Oscar-winning director, and her husband, Wylie, 86, were traveling to Oregon on a hot day. Their deaths are under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight and Madaleine Rubin

item.title: Vance Delays Trip to Switzerland for Direct Talks on Iran Deal
item.description: Mr. Vance said earlier at a press briefing that he did not know if he would travel to Switzerland on Friday for the talks, where he was initially expected for a signing ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: The White House late Thursday said Vice President JD Vance was delaying a trip to Switzerland for negotiations with Iran.

item.title: Union Behind California Billionaire Tax Offers to Reduce the Rate
item.description: The health care workers’ union that made it to the ballot with its plan to impose a 5 percent tax on billionaires’ wealth told Gov. Gavin Newsom it would accept a 2 percent levy instead.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.category: Service Employees International Union-United Healthcare Workers West
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California has opposed the billionaires’ tax, saying that it would hurt the state’s economy.

item.title: Firm Tied to Trump Donor Got No-Bid Contract to Clean Reflecting Pool
item.description: A White House spokeswoman said the president was not involved in selecting Greenwater Services, the business owned by a trust led by John J. Cafaro.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peeled blue paint floating in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on Thursday.

item.title: Trump’s Intelligence Pick Renews Debate: Is the Director of National Intelligence Needed?
item.description: The director of national intelligence’s office was created in the aftermath of the Sept. 11 attacks. Now some lawmakers want to shrink it.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard, seen at a hearing in March, is stepping down as the director of the office of national intelligence, a role that critics say has become too sprawling.

item.title: James Talarico Attacks Ken Paxton Over Child Abuse Case
item.description: The case, which resulted in a short jail sentence despite years of alleged abuse, has drawn outcry in Texas. So have the attempts to politicize it.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Callaghan O'Hare for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Talarico, the Democratic nominee for Senate in Texas, held a news conference to call on Ken Paxton to release files related to Adam Hoffman in Waco, Texas, on Thursday.

item.title: Vance’s Defense of Iran Deal Rests on Vague and Misleading Claims
item.description: The vice president said the United States had leverage to dictate the outcome of the next round of negotiations. But he claimed incorrectly that Iran got no new benefit from the lifting of oil sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Rebecca F. Elliott and Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance during a briefing at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: During Trump’s Immigration Crackdown, Minnesota Protester Remained Shackled in Hospital for Days
item.description: The story of one progressive activist arrested in Minnesota in January shows what critics say is the aggressive nature of the Trump administration’s response to those who have protested its immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Tim Gruber for The New York Times
item.media:description: Paul Johnson, a longtime progressive activist, in the van where he alleges that federal agents assaulted him in January before leaving him shackled to a hospital bed for days before he was charged.

item.title: Trump Redirects Millions From Secret Service Amid White House Construction
item.description: The White House insists that private donations are paying for a new ballroom, but the administration is using taxpayer money for a series of security upgrades.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Construction on the White House grounds in May. President Trump has linked his ballroom project to national security and an underground bunker at the White House.

item.title: Here’s What Barack and Michelle Obama Said at His Presidential Center Opening
item.description: The Obamas each took to the microphone, delivering speeches before a crowd of dignitaries, elected officials and Democratic supporters.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.category: Obama, Barack H, Presidential Library and Museum
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Former President Barack Obama onstage at the grand opening ceremony for the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago on Thursday.

item.title: Three Medals of Honor Awarded to Vietnam and Afghanistan Veterans
item.description: Two Force Recon Marines received the medal on Thursday for their actions more than 50 years ago, alongside an Army officer who showed exceptional bravery in 2012.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
United States Army Special Forces (Green Berets)
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: Maj. James Capers Jr. with President Trump after receiving his Medal of Honor at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Large Waves Sweep Napping Beachgoers in California Into the Sea
item.description: Two college students lying on a Santa Cruz beach were swept as massive waves and dangerous rip currents inundated the coastline in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins and Amy Graff
item.media:credit: Cal Fire SZU San Mateo-Santa Cruz Unit
item.media:description: A Cal Fire rescue helicopter lifted off, after an ocean rescue at Panther Beach and Yellow Bank Beach in Santa Cruz, Calif.

item.title: Colorado Governor Overruled His Own Clemency Board to Release Tina Peters
item.description: Two members of Gov. Jared Polis’s board that reviews commutation requests came forward to say the secretive panel had twice rejected the release of Tina Peters, convicted of election tampering.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy
item.media:credit: Chet Strange for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hannah Proff and Azra Taslimi, two Denver lawyers who serve on Colorado’s 11-person clemency advisory board, say the board twice rejected requests to release Tina Peters.

item.title: Man Charged With Hate Crime Over Burning Cross in Chicago Park
item.description: A former university student, Merlin Lu, 21, has claimed that he set the cross on fire in Grant Park to protest the Trump administration, not as an act of racism.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.media:credit: Google Maps
item.media:description: The intersection of Balbo and Columbus Drives in Chicago, near where a burning cross was found in Grant Park on June 9.

item.title: Report Tallies 39 Adults Who Could Have Helped Save Girls at Camp Mystic
item.description: The finding was part of a growing case against the camp’s leadership, which state investigators said did not organize an evacuation in time “despite ample opportunity.”
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Brandon Bell/Getty Images
item.media:description: The Eastland family speaking to legislators during a hearing on Camp Mystic at the Texas State Capitol in Austin in April.

item.title: Tariq El Sawah, Guantánamo Prisoner Sent to Bosnia, Dies at 68
item.description: He spent nearly 14 years in U.S. custody and never had a day in court even as his deteriorating health drew scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Bryan Denton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tariq El Sawah in 2016, the year his story was included in a New York Times investigation into the mental health damage afflicting military detainees at Guantánamo Bay and elsewhere.

item.title: Scores Fall Ill at Air Force Base After Hegseth Makes Flu Vaccine Optional
item.description: The defense secretary described the vaccine requirement, which he lifted in April, as an “absurd, overreaching” mandate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Maggie Haberman
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Lackland Air Force Base (San Antonio, Tex)
item.media:credit: Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images
item.media:description: Airmen at Lackland Air Force Base last year. A flu outbreak at the base has raced through a training wing among new recruits.

item.title: Takeaways From ‘Regime Change,’ Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan’s Book on Trump’s White House
item.description: The book by Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan of The New York Times, “Regime Change,” reveals a host of details and surprising exchanges as President Trump pushed to drastically expand his power.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump (Book)
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new book by two New York Times reporters describes President Trump’s efforts to bend the federal government, cultural institutions and news cycles to his will.

item.title: Philadelphia Can’t Force Mention of Slavery at Historic Home, Court Rules
item.description: A three-judge appeals panel threw out a lower court’s order requiring the federal government to restore a memorial and historical display at George Washington’s former house.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times
item.media:description: Visitors at the President’s House in Philadelphia in April, a site that focuses heavily on George Washington’s ownership of slaves.

item.title: L.A.P.D. Officer Kills Knicks Fan’s Dog After Celebration
item.description: Marie Marseille had waited her entire life to see the New York Knicks win the N.B.A. championship. Her elation ended in tragedy.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango

item.title: ICE Spent $700 Million on 7 Warehouses. Now It Wants to Get Rid of Them.
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement plans to sell or give away most of the 11 warehouses it bought to detain migrants, reversing course on a signature initiative.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:credit: Rachel Wisniewski for The New York Times
item.media:description: The warehouse that ICE recently purchased for $87 million in Hamburg, Pa.

item.title: Erika Kirk’s Message for Women at Turning Point USA
item.description: Our reporter Vivian Yee details what she saw at this year’s Turning Point USA Women’s Leadership Summit in San Antonio.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Yee, Christina Shaman, Lauren Pruitt, James Surdam and Melanie Bencosme

item.title: Many World Cup Fans Would Rather Skip the Hydration Breaks
item.description: It’s hot in North America in June, but fans are griping that the breaks that have been added to each half are an excuse for the networks to show more ads.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: Even in Toronto, World Cup play is paused once each half for a “hydration break.”

item.title: McCarthy Aide’s Tell-All Book Recounts Trump’s Expletive-Filled Threats to G.O.P.
item.description: A new book by John Leganski, the floor manager for former Speaker Kevin McCarthy, offers a rare behind-the-scenes account of how President Trump wields his power through threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.category: Glory, Grief, and the Gavel: An Inside Guide to Running for Speaker of the House (Book)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: John Leganski, in an orange tie, with Representative Kevin McCarthy when he was elected speaker of the House in January 2023.

item.title: Democratic Socialist Defeats Centrist in the D.C. Mayoral Primary
item.description: President Trump’s grip on the capital loomed over the race, and he threatened to tighten it if Councilwoman Janeese Lewis George is elected. She almost certainly will be.
item.dc:creator: Clyde McGrady

item.title: Supreme Court Narrows Law Banning Drug Users From Owning Guns
item.description: The justices sided with a Texas gun owner who faced criminal charges after admitting to marijuana use argued that a federal gun law violated the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.category: New York State Rifle & Pistol Association v Bruen (Supreme Court Decision)
item.media:credit: Shelby Tauber for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Hemani’s suburban home was raided by federal agents in August 2022 after his family had come under suspicion because of its ties to Iran.

item.title: How Extreme Athletes Accept That They’re ‘Going to Lose a Lot’ of Friends
item.description: Often considered thrill seekers looking for the next adrenaline hit, participants say they’re actually drawn to control and a sense of time slowing down.
item.dc:creator: Kurt Streeter and Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs
item.media:credit: Austin Johnson/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The aftermath of a crash involving a skydiving aircraft in Butler, Mo., on Sunday.

item.title: She Witnessed the Deadly Skydiving Crash, Then Jumped Again
item.description: Lacy Reynolds was waiting to catch the next flight when the aircraft crashed in Butler, Mo., killing 11 passengers and a pilot. She wasn’t sure she could keep skydiving.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto and Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs
item.media:credit: Mid America News Review, via Associated Press

item.title: Student Cheating Is Becoming Impossible to Detect in an A.I. Era
item.description: Big tech companies and small start-ups are using social media to hype new tools that allow students to trick teachers and A.I. detectors.
item.dc:creator: Dana Goldstein

item.title: Barack Obama Presidential Center Opens in Chicago
item.description: Thursday’s opening drew four former presidents, as well as Bruce Springsteen, Bono and Jennifer Hudson. Chicago has seen years of planning and legal fights.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.category: Obama, Barack H, Presidential Library and Museum
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Obama family onstage at the Obama Presidential Center opening ceremony in Chicago on Thursday.
